REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE (TRANSACTION ID # 57355933) FILED BY DEFENDANT HWANG, YOUNG SOON DBA YOCUP YOGURT AND INDIVIDUALLY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT YOUNG HWANG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS IS DENIED. "WHEN A DEFENDANT CHALLENGES THE COURT'S PERSONAL JURISDICTION ON THE GROUND OF IMPROPER SERVICE OF PROCESS THE BURDEN IS ON THE PLAINTIFF TO PROVE...THE FACTS REQUISITE TO AN EFFECTIVE SERVICE." (SUMMERS V. MCCLANAHAN (2006) 140 CAL.APP.4TH 403, 413.) PLAINTIFF HAS ESTABLISHED THAT SUBSTITUTE SERVICE WAS PROPERLY EFFECTUATED. THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED SHOWS THAT DEFENDANT AND SUNG HWAN HWANG OWNED THE PROPERTY IN SARATOGA WHERE THE PAPERS WERE SERVED. (SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF TONY KLEIN, PP. 3-4.) THE INDIVIDUAL WHO WAS GIVEN THE PAPERS IDENTIFIED HIMSELF AS SUNG HWAN HWANG, DEFENDANT'S HUSBAND AND THEREFORE A COMPETENT MEMBER OF THE HOUSEHOLD. (ID., P. 4-5.) THIS IS SUFFICIENT TO MEET PLAINTIFF'S BURDEN, PARTICULARLY IN LIGHT OF THE FACT THAT DEFENDANT HAS SUBMITTED NO ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE ABOUT WHERE DEFENDANT RESIDES OR WHETHER SUNG HWAN HWANG IS HER HUSBAND. THE DECLARATIONS OF MICHAEL HWANG ARE NOT EXECUTED UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY AND CAN THEREFORE NOT BE CONSIDERED. (SEE CCP SEC. 2015.5.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT YOUNG HWANG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS IS CONTINUED TO JULY 13, 2015 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JUNE 15, 2015 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jul-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT YOUNG HWANG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is denied. "When a defendant challenges the court's personal jurisdiction on the ground of improper service of process the burden is on the plaintiff to prove...the facts requisite to an effective service." (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 413.) Plaintiff has established that substitute service was properly effectuated. The evidence submitted shows that Defendant and Sung Hwan Hwang owned the property in Saratoga where the papers were served. (Supplemental Declaration of Tony Klein, pp. 3-4.) The individual who was given the papers identified himself as Sung Hwan Hwang, Defendant's husband and therefore a competent member of the household. (Id., p. 4-5.) This is sufficient to meet Plaintiff's burden, particularly in light of the fact that Defendant has submitted no admissible evidence about where Defendant resides or whether Sung Hwan Hwang is her husband. The Declarations of Michael Hwang are not executed under penalty of perjury and can therefore not be considered. (See CCP sec. 2015.5.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jun-15-2015 Continued To Jul-13-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT YOUNG HWANG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is continued to July 13, 2015 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than June 15, 2015 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR # 7489 =302/hk
